Place of flu and Tdap vaccination among pregnant women was most often the office of an obstetrician/gynecologist (OB/GYN) or midwife (flu: 40.0%; Tdap: 72.4%). Place of COVID-19 vaccination was most often a family or other physician's office (22.4%). 
Figure 3. Main reasons for not receiving flu vaccine (n=802), * Tdap (n=322), † or updated 2023–2024 COVID-19 vaccine (n=1,316) ‡ among unvaccinated pregnant women — Internet panel survey, United States, April 2024 
View Larger Download 
The most frequently reported main reasons for not receiving flu vaccination before or during pregnancy were concern about the safety of their baby if they were to get vaccinated (14.7%), and just not wanting the vaccine (14.0%) 
View Larger Download 
The most common main reason for not receiving Tdap during pregnancy was that they already got the Tdap vaccine (during a previous pregnancy or at another time) (19.5%). 
View Larger Download 
The most common main reason for not receiving an updated 2023–2024 COVID-19 vaccine before or during pregnancy was just not wanting the vaccine (16.3%). 
* Among women pregnant during October 2023–January 2024 who reported not receiving a flu vaccination before or during pregnancy (n=856), 1 respondent did not ans
